now that we have nip29 support we should have the singleton daemon check which nip29 groups we own at all times (i.e. leave the subscription open), and when we start a session in a new project that doesn't have a nip29 group yet we should autocreate the group automatically. We should also auto-add members to the group whenever we initiate a session with a new agent (so that whenever the agent wants to publish their presence status the agent finds itself already added to the group) -- this membership events should be signed with the userNsec key in .tenex/config.json

Implemented NIP-29 group ownership in the singleton daemon: a persistent subscription tracks owned groups, groups are auto-created on session start, and new agents are auto-added as members before the engine starts — all signed with userNsec. Also fixed a cache-poisoning bug where relay rejections were treated as success, and verified the topology where admin-signed events are accepted over non-member connections.
